prompt: I just noticed our django-app/.env file might have been committed by accident a few weeks ago. Can you check if we have any secrets leaked in the git history and also check if our current .env files are properly gitignored? Also check the .env for any default/placeholder values that shouldn't be in production.

assertions.description: A security-report.md file is written to outputs/
Git history is actually inspected (runs git log or git grep commands)
Checks that .env files are properly gitignored
Provides specific remediation steps (git commands or config changes), not just general advice
